data.body: ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED via the change-369 diagnostic stderr (both CI signatures now captured): git add fails creating the loose-object temp under the tracker's shared .git/objects/ — ENOENT on Linux ('unable to create temporary file: No such file or directory'), EINVAL on macOS ('… Invalid argument') — then 'failed to insert into database' / 'unable to index file' / 'fatal: adding files failed'. NOT gc (154 objects « 6700, already refuted) and NOT index.lock/HEAD; no in-process concurrent git actor exists (xdist absent → serial; _fresh_repo has no origin remote → no background push). It is a transient runner-FS fault the write path surfaced as a hard StoreError. FIX (Gerrit change 484, fix/store-transient-git-add-retry): route every git add in event_append.py (stage_and_commit + batch_stage_and_commit + UU recovery) through a _git_add helper that retries 3x ONLY on that transient signature; any other add failure still fails on the first attempt. RED→GREEN test tests/interfaces/store/test_event_append_transient_retry.py (mutation-checked). Will close when change 484 is Verified +1.
